name: Foothill Gate
short_desc: An ivy-choked stone arch marks the start of the old keep-road, bells hanging silent.
long_desc: The bells are dwarven work — three of them, one cracked, all fouled with old cobweb. Someone has left offerings at the base of the arch: a scrap of steel, a wedge of dry cheese, a child's tin soldier with the paint gone. Beyond the arch the path climbs out of sight.

name: Gate Terrace
short_desc: A wind-scoured terrace before a pair of iron-bound doors, arrow-slits above.
long_desc: The gate is barred from inside and braced with a fresh beam; the flagstones are chipped where something heavy struck and struck again. Dwarven faces are watching through the arrow-slits, counting your weapons before they decide whether to open a wicket.

name: Forge Heart
short_desc: The clan-forge burns hotter than the siege demands, a reddish light filling the vault.
long_desc: The great bellows sigh in threes and the anvils are never cold. Ingots are stacked in neat pyramids that shouldn't be possible under siege — someone has been working through the nights. Above the hearth hangs a hammer too old and too large to be swung by any hand alive.

name: Scree Watchpost
short_desc: A stone pillbox half-buried in loose rock, its firing-slits facing downslope.
long_desc: The slope below is lined with splintered shields and a great many arrows snapped at the haft, the way they break when they hit something they shouldn't have. A horn hangs on a peg inside; the rim is dented as if someone, once, hit a besieger with it.

name: Field of Standards
short_desc: A stand of tall poles, each topped with a painted skull, casting no shadows.
long_desc: There are more standards than can reasonably belong to one chieftain, and the paint on the farthest ones is very old indeed. The wind moves across them in a pattern — tk, tk, tk — that sounds like fingernails on a door, and none of the skulls have lower jaws.

name: Throne of Bone
short_desc: A stone hall, a throne built of jaw-bones, and a man seated in it who is eating.
long_desc: The chieftain's hall is colder than the mountain outside. The throne is a mosaic of lower jaws, set in a pattern that is almost but not quite writing. Behind it, a banner of human hair. Before it, a long table laid with one plate, which is full, and which refills by a degree each time he takes a bite.
